question.text: If InvIT units are not listed or money is not refunded within the specified time, what rate of interest per annum must the InvIT pay?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under InvIT Regulations, InvITs shall pay interest at 15% per annum from the closure of the issue in case the Units are not listed or the money refunded within the specified time. **The Trap:** 12% and 18% are common interest rates in other financial contexts but the InvIT regulation specifies exactly 15%. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 15% interest penalty is a strong deterrent against delays in listing or refunds, protecting investor interests. The same rate applies to REITs as well.
